description:
Choropleth map displaying geographic locations of events or features, represented by grey circles, across the Balkan Peninsula and surrounding seas. The map covers Slovenia, Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Serbia, Romania, Bulgaria, Albania, Greece, and parts of Italy. Major water bodies labelled include the Adriatic Sea, Tyrrhenian Sea, Ionian Sea, Sea of Crete, and Mediterranean Sea. Countries are depicted in light grey, and sea areas in a slightly darker grey. Red lines mark geological boundaries, likely tectonic plate boundaries or fault lines, primarily within the marine areas. Grey circles with black outlines are distributed across the map, with their size varying, suggesting a difference in magnitude or intensity, though no legend is provided to confirm this.

Notable concentrations of circles occur in:
*   The Ionian Sea, off the coast of Italy and Greece.
*   Along the coastline of Albania and Greece, extending into the Aegean Sea and the Sea of Crete.
*   Off the coast of Croatia in the Adriatic Sea.
*   In Romania.

A large circle is visible off the heel of Italy, and several large circles are present in the Ionian Sea and south of the Sea of Crete. Many circles are spatially correlated with the red geological boundary lines. No legend, scale bar, compass, or specific date is visible.
